title: Morgan Stanley says Indian stock market poised for strong year ahead. Here’s why
summary: Morgan Stanley remains bullish on Indian equities, citing an earnings upcycle, supportive macroeconomic conditions and strong domestic flows. The brokerage expects sustained growth driven by investment, manufacturing and AI-linked opportunities, while highlighting external risks. It projects the Sensex could reach 89,000, supported by robust earnings growth and improving market fundamentals.

title: ​NMDC among 4 stocks closed crossing above VWAP
summary: Four stocks from the Nifty500 universe closed more than 1% above their VWAP on June 1, signalling strong intraday buying interest and improving sentiment. Closing above the Volume Weighted Average Price is considered a bullish indicator, suggesting sustained demand, accumulation by market participants and the potential continuation of upward price momentum.

title: Coal India among 5 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
summary: On June 01, five Nifty200 stocks were highlighted in the RSI Trending Up scan based on data from StockEdge.com. The signal is generated when RSI crosses above 50 from lower levels, indicating improving momentum. Rising RSI values suggest strengthening price action and potential bullish continuation, making the indicator useful for traders tracking possible buying opportunities.

title: Yaashvi Jewellers shares to list today. Check GMP ahead of debut
summary: Yaashvi Jewellers' shares are poised for their debut on the BSE SME platform. The initial public offering is expected to see a subdued listing performance. Grey market indicators suggest the stock will trade near its issue price. The company, established in 2016, manufactures and trades gold jewellery. It has reported strong financial growth in recent years.

title: Bonus issue alert! Last day to buy Anand Rathi Wealth shares for 1:1 bonus reward. Do you own?
summary: Anand Rathi Wealth shareholders are set to receive bonus shares in a 1:1 ratio, with today being the last chance to buy for eligibility. The company, reporting strong Q4 FY26 results with a 40.5% profit jump and significant AUM growth, is capitalizing reserves for this move. Bonus shares will be credited by June 7.

title: Negative Breakout: These 9 stocks cross below their 200 DMAs
summary: Nine stocks flashed bearish signals after falling below their 200-day moving averages, indicating weakening momentum and potential downside risk.

title: Dalal Street set to open lower amid sharp decline in GIFT Nifty
summary: Indian equities faced pressure on Monday, mirroring Friday's weakness, with Nifty declining 0.7%. Analysts anticipate a range-bound market with a slight negative bias due to persistent FII selling and global uncertainties. Focus is expected to shift to stock-specific opportunities in the midcap segment.

title: Stocks in news: Ola Electric, Eicher Motors, Axis Bank, Anant Raj, Wipro
summary: Markets faced pressure on Monday, with key indices declining over half a percent due to weak global cues. Ola Electric approved its QIP issue, Eicher Motors reported a 15% sales rise, and Axis Bank increased its stake in Axis Max Life Insurance.

title: Sebi to relax InvIT cash flow distribution rules
summary: Sebi has proposed a relaxation in the net distributable cash flow calculation for road sector InvITs. The regulator will allow debt-funded major maintenance expenses to be added back, addressing industry concerns that current rules discourage project monetization through InvITs.

title: Rules in the works to enable G-Sec, repo deals by insurers
summary: Insurers will soon be able to undertake repo and government securities lending transactions. The insurance regulator is developing an operational framework for these activities. This follows recent amendments to insurance laws. These changes offer insurers greater flexibility in managing their liquidity. Insurers hold a significant portion of government securities. The new framework will detail settlement, platforms, collateral, and risk management.

title: Zaynich's blockbuster potential lifts Wockhardt's long-term outlook
summary: Wockhardt's novel antibiotic, Zaynich, has received USFDA approval after a 12-year effort, sparking hopes for a turnaround after years of net losses. The drug, targeting multidrug-resistant bacteria, has a projected peak sales of $1.5 billion, potentially transforming the company's financial outlook. Investors have responded positively, with the stock surging on the news.

title: Ola Electric opens QIP, sets floor price at Rs 37.74/share
summary: Ola Electric Mobility has approved its qualified institutional placement (QIP), setting a floor price of ₹37.74 per equity share. This fundraising initiative aims to bolster the company amidst slowing sales and market share pressures in the electric two-wheeler sector. The move follows a recent ₹2,000 crore investment into its subsidiaries for enhanced localization and vertical integration.

title: IT stocks rally as AI deals, valuations draw investors
summary: Indian IT stocks saw a significant surge on Monday, outperforming the broader market. The Nifty IT index reached its highest point since April 23. This rally was driven by attractive stock valuations and new AI partnerships. Investors are showing renewed interest, building fresh long positions. This rebound follows a period of underperformance for the IT sector this year.

title: CBDT tells tax officers to tighten scrutiny of unexplained income, assets
summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes has directed tax officers to be more careful and consistent when using rules against hidden income and assets. This follows a report highlighting inconsistent application that cost the government revenue. These rules help tax authorities treat unexplained money, investments, or spending as income if taxpayers cannot provide a valid source.

title: RBI reviews expected credit loss rules amid concerns over impact on SME credit costs
summary: The Reserve Bank of India is taking a closer look at its credit loss regulations, which may result in higher borrowing costs for small and mid-sized firms. With rating agencies recently exceeding default rate benchmarks, there is a chance of elevated risk weights for these businesses.

title: JLR to raise $2 billion five-year loan from global banks to refinance debt
summary: Jaguar Land Rover has announced a pivotal financial strategy, obtaining a $2 billion loan from leading multinational banks. This endeavor serves to refinance debts that are maturing early next year. With the loan priced at 155 basis points above the SONIA rate, it reflects a savvy approach amidst current market uncertainties.

title: Nearly two-thirds of bank loans now priced below 9% as rate cuts bite
summary: Indian banks are offering more loans at lower interest rates. Almost two-thirds of all bank credit now has rates below 9%. This shift follows a year of aggressive rate cuts. While credit growth is strong, banks' profit margins face pressure. Metropolitan areas and public sector banks lead lending. Agriculture and industrial credit also accelerated. Personal loan growth moderated.

title: Oil prices ease as uncertainty clouds Iran-US peace talks
summary: Crude prices surged on Monday as the renewed tensions revived fears that disruptions to global oil supplies could persist for a long time.

title: Gold Holds Decline as Traders Weigh Confusion Over US-Iran Talks
summary: Gold held a decline as conflicting signals from the US and Iran cast doubt over a diplomatic resolution to the war, fanning concerns over inflation and prolonged trade disruptions.

title: Go Colors eyes bigger stores, broader apparel play for growth reset
summary: Go Colors eyes a shift from a women's bottomwear brand to a broad apparel retailer, with a pilot run of larger stores and diverse categories. Despite recent struggles in sales, the company is optimistic about growth, particularly in menswear, and is focusing on refining its portfolio.

title: Cyient’s largest acquisition puts spotlight on buyouts by mid-sized IT companies to boost revenue
summary: Acquisitions have taken the shopping bills of the country’s 15 largest IT services companies to $5 billion since April last year, helping them to increase revenue.

title: Tata Sons marks down the valuation of Tata Neu’s parent during latest cash infusion
summary: When Tata Sons invested again in Tata Digital this year, it was at a lower valuation. Tata's effort to build an everything-app through Tata Digital has resulted in nearly  ₹17,000 crore in losses, after investments of over  ₹26,000 crore over seven years.

title: NVIDIA salaries revealed: Software engineers can earn up to  ₹3.74 crore as AI giant expands H-1B hiring
summary: Nvidia is stepping up H-1B hiring even as rivals scale back. Federal filings reveal salaries for software engineers, AI researchers, product managers and directors, with pay reaching  ₹4.67 crore.

title: More companies slipped into losses in Q4. Is the worst still ahead?
summary: More companies turned loss-making in Q4FY26 despite stronger revenue growth, as rising raw material costs exposed smaller businesses with limited pricing power. Analysts warn the pressure could intensify in FY27 as higher crude prices flow through new inventories and into corporate margins.

title: Wockhardt’s new drug, India’s second to get US nod, now eyes commercial success
summary: The nod for Zaynich—an antibiotic for treatment of complicated UTI—vindicates chairman and founder Habil Khorakiwala’s bet on antibiotic discovery and development, even as Big Pharma companies in developed economies stopped such research in favour of more lucrative drugs.

title: Companies turn to private market deals amid IPO slowdown, says Deloitte's Berry
summary: While companies continue to believe in India's long-term growth story, many are choosing to wait out the current turbulence before tapping public markets, creating opportunities for private equity, private credit and strategic investors.
